title: Aetanthus holtonii
intro: Aetanthus holtonii, also known as the Holton's aetanthus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The bioactive constituents of Aetanthus holtonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aetanthus holtonii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and colds.
